data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: SAVE, SBUX, MCD, QCOM, and more
data.description:
Stock futures continued to edge lower, exhibiting a cautious move on Wednesday ahead of the most anticipated earnings of an EV maker, Tesla (TSLA).

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Texas Instruments (TXN) shares climbed nearly 4% after the company exceeded Q3 expectations, with revenue showing a 9% sequential improvement. For Q4, the company forecasts earnings between $1.07 and $1.29 per share and revenue between $3.7B and $4B. Analysts, however, had projected earnings of $1.35 per share and $4.08B in revenue, both above the midpoint of Texas Instruments' guidance.
  • Spirit Airlines (SAVE) advanced 29% following reports of early-stage merger talks with Frontier Group (ULCC), potentially as part of Spirit's debt restructuring through bankruptcy. The merger discussions, if successful, could provide operational synergies and financial stability, helping Spirit manage its liabilities. This news comes on the heels of a 53% increase in Spirit's share price on Monday after securing a debt-refinancing extension with its credit card processor.
  • Trump Media & Technology Group (DJT) continued its upward momentum on Wednesday, climbing an additional 5% as investors speculated on former President Donald Trump's increasing chances of winning the upcoming November election, now less than two weeks away. This follows a strong performance on Tuesday, when the stock reached its highest level since July, closing with a nearly 10% gain.

Biggest stock losers

  • Enphase Energy (ENPH) shares tumbled 12% after the company posted disappointing Q3 results, with a 31% Y/Y revenue decline and net income dropping to $45.76M, or $0.33/share, from $113.95M, or $0.80/share, a year ago. For Q4, Enphase provided downbeat guidance, projecting revenue between $360M-$400M, a midpoint of $380M below the consensus of $433.5M. The company expects non-GAAP gross margins between 49%-52% with net IRA benefit and 39%-42% excluding it.
  • Despite increasing its dividend by 7%, Starbucks (SBUX) shares dropped over 4% due to disappointing preliminary fourth-quarter results. Global comparable store sales decreased by 7%, and consolidated net revenue declined 3% to $9.1B, below the $9.4B consensus. Non-GAAP EPS came in at $0.80, missing the $1.03 consensus and down from $1.06 last year. This decline was primarily attributed to weaker performance in North America, particularly in the United States, where comparable store sales declined by 6% due to fewer customer transactions. Additionally, Starbucks faced challenges in China, with comparable store sales declining 14% due to heightened competition and a sluggish economic environment. Given the CEO transition and current business conditions, the company has also suspended its financial guidance for the entire FY2025.
  • McDonald's (MCD) shares fell as much as 6% following a warning issued by the Centers for Disease Control (CDC) regarding an E. coli outbreak in Colorado and Nebraska potentially connected to the restaurant chain's Quarter Pounder. Initial investigations suggest that a portion of the illnesses may be attributed to slivered onions used in the Quarter Pounder, sourced from a specific supplier serving three distribution centers. In response, McDonald's has taken immediate safety measures by removing the affected product from its supply chain and pausing the distribution of all slivered onions in the impacted region. Furthermore, the company has temporarily discontinued the sale of Quarter Pounders in restaurants located in Colorado, Kansas, Utah, Wyoming, and parts of several other states.
  • Qualcomm's (QCOM) shares slipped more than 5% after reports surfaced that Arm Holdings decided to terminate a key license agreement that granted the chipmaker access to Arm's intellectual property for chip design. The British chip designer, a longtime partner and key supplier to Qualcomm, issued a 60-day cancellation notice, intensifying their ongoing legal battle. This move escalates an ongoing legal dispute between the two companies, which began in 2022 when Arm sued Qualcomm for breaching contract terms and trademark infringement after Qualcomm acquired Nuvia, a chip-making startup that was also an Arm licensee. Qualcomm argues that the current license covers Nuvia’s operations, while Arm insists that a renegotiation was required after the acquisition.

More on related stocks:

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow slip, yields tick higher as investors await Tesla earnings
data.description:
The major averages slipped on Wednesday, a day after the S&P 500 index posted back-to-back negative sessions for the first time since early September. Investors will now turn their attention to Tesla (TSLA) earnings.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.4%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.8% and the Dow (DJI) was -0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.25%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.06%.

Wall Street ended in a mixed fashion on Tuesday, with the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite finishing higher.

"The markets are being weighed down by the recent increase in bond rates and declining hope on the direction of the interest rate," Patrick Munnelly, from Tickmill Group, said.

"With less than two weeks to go before the Nov. 5 election, investors are bracing for volatility in the markets," Munnelly added.

Investors will also keep their eyes on Tesla's (TSLA) Q3 earnings report on Wednesday, which will land after the closing bell.

On the economic front, September existing home sales data unexpectedly dipped as prices climb. U.S. existing home sales fell 1.0% M/M in September to 3.84M.

The Fed's Beige Book will be released later in the day.

data.title: Existing home sales unexpectedly dip in September as prices climb
data.description: • U.S. existing home sales fell 1.0% M/M in September to 3.84M, worse than the 3.88M consensus and August's 3.88M (revised from 3.86M), the National Association of Realtors said in a Wednesday report.

data.title: Tupperware Brands to form The New Tupperware Company amid struggles
data.description:
Florida-based Tupperware Brands (OTC:TUPBQ) announced an agreement to form a new entity called The New Tupperware Company.

This decision comes after the company faced challenges related to an over-leveraged balance sheet and an outdated operational model.

The deal is structured as a private sale of all intellectual property necessary for creating and marketing Tupperware's (OTC:TUPBQ) products, along with operating assets in the U.S. and other international subsidiaries.

As part of this transition, Tupperware (OTC:TUPBQ) will wind down operations in markets that carry heavy liabilities.

"Winding down parts of the Company will be a difficult but necessary decision to protect the future of the Tupperware brand. I want to thank all the wonderful people that will always be a part of the Tupperware family. Change and disruption are challenging, but at times, needed to move forward." said Laurie Ann Goldman, President and CEO.

The agreement involves a group of secured lenders, including Stonehill Capital Management Partners and Alden Global Capital, who will purchase the brand and invest in its future growth.

The transaction closing expected by October end.

data.title: Magnificent 7 Q3 Preview: All eyes on AI market gains as big tech earnings loom
data.description:
As large-cap growth (Magnificent 7) stocks continue to dominate S&P 500 (SP500) performance, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) will kick off third-quarter earnings this week, while Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL), Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), and Meta (NASDAQ:META), will be among those reporting next week.

Most of the Mag 7s have seen their stocks clock double-digit percentage gains this year, barring Tesla (TSLA), which has been the lone decliner, with shares sliding 12.6% year-to-date.

The percentage of investors that are overweight the Magnificent 7 has, meanwhile, declined by 10% according to the Jefferies TMT Trading October 2024 Positioning Survey.

Investors would, therefore, closing watch year-ahead earnings forecasts and investments into artificial intelligence, which has driven much of the recent earnings gains.

Here is a quick recap of the performance and sentiment around the stocks:

Electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) is scheduled to release its Q3 financial results on Wednesday after the market closes, with analysts anticipating a 9% decline in profits despite expected top-line growth of about 10%. For investors, Elon Musk’s commentary on the timing of a new mass-market cheaper electric vehicle, energy storage business, and robotaxi developments will be of keen interest.

Tesla's (TSLA) investments in self-driving technology are reaching a point where significant progress will be needed for the company to maintain its valuation premium. A consensus of analysts expects the company to report earnings per share of $0.60 on revenue of $25.67B.

Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) will be next on deck, with its report scheduled for Tuesday, Oct. 29. Having shed about 9% in value in the July-September period, the tech giant is trading at a significant discount to its peers, and the focus will be on its antitrust issues despite strong performance in the previous quarter.

Microsoft (MSFT), down 5.8% in the July – September period, while Meta (META), up 13% in the same period, are slated to report on Wednesday, Oct. 30.

According to Piper Sandler, Microsoft's upcoming earnings report could be mixed due to new metrics published in August that are not yet fully reflected in analysts' estimates, including a material change to Azure. Microsoft updated its fiscal year 2025 investor metrics in August, which included several significant segment changes. Morgan Stanley said that the reporting changes made by Microsoft should provide a "cleaner" look at its all-important Azure segment.

A consensus of analysts expects the company to report earnings per share of $3.10 on revenue of $64.54B.

Meanwhile, for Facebook parent Meta (META), analysts expect earnings per share of $5.29 on revenue of $40.29B, with a primary focus on the AI market, and the company’s AI monetization.

The company's potential growth engines include AI and AR/VR, but the market has already priced in much of its future potential, suggesting a low margin of safety, writes one SA author. Meta (META) has recently initiated layoffs across various divisions, specifically targeting teams within WhatsApp, Instagram, and Reality Labs. The company gets a Seeking Alpha quant rating of 'Hold' with the highest factor grades marked for profitability.

When it comes to Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) consensus estimate for its FQ4 revenue is $94.23B, representing ~5% Y/Y growth. EPS estimate is $1.55. SA columnist Daniel Jones writes upcoming financial results on October 31st "show revenue growth, particularly in services, but weaknesses in iPhone and wearables sales, and challenges in China persist."

Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), which reports on the same day, is expected to post a nearly 21% growth in profit, with revenue rising 9.9%. Brokerage JMP continues to favor AMZN for its resilient upper-funnel trends, exposure to non-discretionary items, its large and growing advertising business, and AWS' exposure to AI; rates AMZN at ‘Market Outperform.’

Chip powerhouse Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) reports in November and is projected to earn $0.74 on revenue of $32.92B, implying a whopping ~82% Y/Y growth in top-line. BofA recently increased Nvidia's earnings estimates for the years ahead, citing the company’s impressive market share (80% to 85%) in a $400B total addressable market.

More on Magnificent 7 stocks:

data.title: Kuehne & Nagel International reports 9M results
data.description:
• Kuehne & Nagel International press release (OTCPK:KHNGF): 9M Net turnover of 18,041M CHF 
  • Gross profit 6,460M CHF 
  • EBITDA 1,846M CHF 
  • EBIT 1,233M CHF 
  • The nine-months result was impacted by negative exchange rate effects of 2%and non-recurring restructuring costs of CHF 17 million booked in thesecond quarter of 2024.

data.title: DSV A/S reports Q3 results
data.description:
• DSV A/S press release (OTCPK:DSDVF): Q3 gross profit improved by 4.8% and EBIT before special items was 1.5% higher compared to the same period last year.
  • Adjusted free cash flow generation in Q3 2024 improved to DKK 2,524 million.
  • Outlook for 2024
  • EBIT before special items is expected to be in the range of DKK 16,000-17,000 million (previously DKK 15,500-17,000 million).
  • The effective tax rate is expected to be approximately 24%.
  • One-off costs (special items) are expected to be in the level of DKK 650 million in 2024.

data.title: Black Mammoth Metals to raise ~$3.2M via private placement
data.description:
• Black Mammoth Metals (TSXV:BMM:CA) is seeking to raise gross proceeds of up to $3.22M by way of a private placement of up to 4.6M shares at a price of $0.70 per unit.
  • Each unit consists of one common share and one-half common share purchase warrant at an exercise price of $1.10.
  • Proceeds to be used for its exploration properties, its America Mine property, its non-core exploration interests, general working capital and for potential acquisitions.

data.title: Zijin Mining Group reports 9M results
data.description:
• Zijin Mining Group press release (OTCPK:ZIJMF): 9M Revenue reached RMB 230.4 billion.
  • Net profit attributable to the parent company of RMB 24.4 billion from Q1 to Q3 2024, reflecting a 51% increase year-over-year.
  • The debt ratio saw a decline of 4.28 percentage points from the beginning of the year.
  • Mining operations posted a gross profit margin of 57.7%, up 7.4 percentage points year-over-year, while the overall gross margin reached 19.5%, an increase of 4.4 percentage points.

data.title: Svenska Handelsbanken AB reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Svenska Handelsbanken AB press release (OTCPK:SVNLF): Q3 Earnings per share grew to SEK 3.64 
  • Operating profit increased to SEK 9,061.
  • The common equity tier 1 ratio was 18.8%.
